title: Review a seeded pagination-fix patch
description: A contributor has proposed a patch that claims to fix the reservation-list pagination symptom (reservations going missing when paging through GET /reservations). The patch is supplied as a unified diff against the starting revision at datasets/development/bk-009/patch.diff in the platform repository. Review it as you would a real pull request: does it fix the symptom, is it correct at the edges, does it follow the repository's conventions, and is it safe to merge as-is? The patch contains at least one seeded defect; your review is expected to find it. Produce a review report whose findings cite file and line in the patched code.
acceptance_criteria: The review report states whether the patch fixes the reported pagination symptom, with reasoning.
Every finding cites file:line against the patched state of the code.
The seeded defect in the patch is identified, with its user-visible consequence explained.
The report ends with a clear recommendation: accept, or request changes with the required changes listed.
The bookit working tree is left unmodified; any application of the patch happens in a disposable worktree or copy.
constraints: Review the patch against the starting revision exactly; do not review a rebased or edited variant.
Applying the patch and running tests in an isolated worktree is allowed and encouraged, but no change may be left behind.
Findings must be evidenced (file:line, test output, or reproduction), not speculative.
non_goals: Writing a corrected version of the patch.
Reviewing pre-existing code the patch does not touch, except where the patch interacts with it.
Style nitpicks that golangci-lint would not flag and AGENTS.md does not require.

visible_validation.description: The supplied patch applies cleanly to the starting revision.
Review report exists with file:line-cited findings and a recommendation.
The target repository working tree is unchanged after the review (empty output).

expected_artifacts: reports/bk-009-review.md: findings with file:line citations and an accept/request-changes recommendation

human_review_rubric.criterion: The seeded defect is found and its consequence is described accurately
Findings are evidenced at file:line and free of hallucinated code
The review exercised the patch (applied it, ran or reasoned through tests) rather than pattern-matching the diff text
No residue left in the target repository
A maintainer could act on the recommendation without re-deriving the analysis

known_ambiguities: The depth of edge-case exploration is open; at minimum, consider what happens across the full range of page and per_page values the API accepts.
Whether a finding is blocking or advisory is the reviewer's call and must be labeled in the report.
expected_failure_modes: Approving the patch because the happy-path pages look correct.
Reporting the pre-existing symptom as if it were introduced by the patch.
